title: Suspicious Polyfill login prompts pop up on Toshiba, Muji websites
description: Tech giant Toshiba and mega-retailer Muji warned visitors that suspicious sign-in screens popping up on their websites could collect credentials. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: CISA: Hackers now exploit SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers
description: CISA warned today that hackers are now actively exploiting a recently patched high-severity SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Chinese APT deploys new malware to keep access to hacked networks
description: A Chinese espionage group tracked as UNC5221 has been accessing Microsoft 365 environments using the Brickstorm backdoor and previously undocumented malware named Plenet and AgentPSD. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Dark web Nemesis Market vendor gets 26 years for selling drugs
description: A California man was sentenced to more than 26 years in federal prison for trafficking fentanyl and methamphetamine through Nemesis Market, one of the world's largest dark web marketplaces. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Over 900 US gas station tank gauge systems exposed to attacks
description: Over 900 automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems across the United States, used to monitor fuel and chemical storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors, have been found exposed online and are vulnerable to ongoing attacks. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: What 2026 DBIR Confirms: Attacks Are Living in the Browser
description: Phishing, shadow AI, malicious extensions, and credential theft increasingly happen inside the browser. Keep Aware explains what the 2026 Verizon DBIR reveals about browser-layer security gaps and modern attacks. [...]
author: Sponsored by Keep Aware
